mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand that drives corporate revenue expectations higher, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX posts negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of months when RSAFS is rising month-over-month, or Rank IC is negative over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, restrictive monetary policy elevates borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days only when both series move together.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises month-over-month and UNRATE falls month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE is followed by negative SPX 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no predictive power versus individual series (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions where unemployment is below 5%, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals overheating that invites monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months with UNRATE below 5% and rising INDPRO, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the negative return prediction is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth quarters are followed by negative SPX 60-day returns more than 50% of the time, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is below 0.03 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,000 thousand units signals collapsing residential investment and negative wealth effects that drag S&P 500 returns lower over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1,000, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered months, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation measured by month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL combined with a federal funds rate above 4% creates a restrictive real policy environment that compresses margins and equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS above 4% is followed by positive SPX 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term does not significantly improve prediction over individual series (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals improving labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with month-over-month declining UNRATE are followed by negative SPX 5-day returns more than 50% of the time, or the positive return spread versus months with rising UNRATE is not significant (t-test p > 0.05).
